M-032.NAME: vpn-network-addressing-capacity
M-032.path: backend/app/core/vpn_network.py backend/app/core/config.py backend/app/vpn/amneziawg.py backend/app/vpn/models.py backend/app/core/init_vpn.py backend/app/vpn/provisioning.py backend/app/vpn/topology.py deploy/lib/vpn-network.sh deploy/deploy-on-server.sh deploy/deploy-all.sh install.sh .env.example .env.production
M-032.owns: Configurable VPN client and RU/DE relay address pools, non-10.0.0.0/8 deployment defaults, capacity validation for 500/1000 client devices, reboot-safe full-tunnel routing/NAT restore, and explicit address-pool migration controls

exports.fn-build-vpn-network-settings: Normalize backend client/relay network settings and validate capacity/overlap constraints
exports.fn-vpn-network-resolve: Deploy helper that preserves existing AWG interface networks unless VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=1
exports.env-VPN_CLIENT_SUBNET: Operator-selected client address pool; recommended fresh-deploy value is 172.29.0.0/22 for up to 1000 devices
exports.env-VPN_CLIENT_GATEWAY: Server-side AWG client-interface address derived from the client subnet, normally 172.29.0.1
exports.env-VPN_RELAY_SUBNET: Operator-selected RU/DE transit pool; recommended fresh-deploy value is 172.29.255.0/30
exports.env-VPN_RELAY_DE_ADDRESS: DE relay interface address inside the transit subnet, normally 172.29.255.1
exports.env-VPN_RELAY_RU_ADDRESS: RU relay client address inside the transit subnet, normally 172.29.255.2
exports.env-VPN_CAPACITY_PROFILE: Validated capacity profile such as 500 or 1000 that rejects too-small client pools
exports.env-VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE: Explicit operator gate for live address-pool migration and client config reprovisioning
exports.fn-vpn-runtime-write-restore-service: Deploy helper that installs krotpn-vpn-runtime.service so RU policy routing, Telegram routes, and RU/DE NAT are restored after host reboot

configuration.default-client-subnet: 172.29.0.0/22 for fresh deployments; legacy upgrades preserve existing configured subnet unless migration is explicit
configuration.headroom-option: /21 when operator wants growth beyond 1000 devices without another tunnel renumbering

decisions.decision-1: Fresh deployments should avoid 10.0.0.0/8 because the operator already uses that supernet for OpenVPN.
decisions.decision-2: /24 pools are invalid for 500/1000-device targets; /22 is the minimum practical 1000-device profile and /21 is the recommended headroom option.
decisions.decision-3: Existing deployments must not silently change client tunnel addresses because AWG configs embed fixed Address values.
decisions.decision-4: Deploy scripts and backend allocation must derive client, relay, NAT, policy-routing and config-generation behavior from one validated network source.
decisions.decision-5: Full-tunnel policy routing and NAT are runtime host state, so deploy must install an explicit systemd restore unit instead of relying only on one-shot iptables/ip rule calls or if-up hooks.

implementation-status.done: Implemented backend VPN network helpers, capacity-aware client allocation, explicit reprovision migration guard, deploy-time network helper, 172.29 fresh-deploy env defaults, reboot-safe krotpn-vpn-runtime.service renderers, and static regression tests.
implementation-status.verification: V-M-032 passed on 2026-05-23 for bash syntax/static gates covering install.sh, deploy scripts and deploy/lib/vpn-network.sh.
implementation-status.live-verification: Operator-confirmed VPS deployment test passed for release v2.13.0-vpn-network-capacity-stable on 2026-04-20; 2026-05-23 live hotfix restored RU/DE runtime routing/NAT after reboot and promoted that behavior into deploy-time systemd restore.

disaster-recovery-role.role-1: Phase-22 restore validation must compare restored VPN_CLIENT_SUBNET, VPN_RELAY_SUBNET, VPN_CAPACITY_PROFILE, and stored VPNClient addresses against the backup manifest.
disaster-recovery-role.role-2: VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE must remain an explicit operator gate for any restore path that would invalidate existing client configs.

M-032.hotspot: H-019: Address-pool changes must be explicit because existing clients need rotated/reprovisioned configs.
